purpose: Provides statistical discipline for confidence ratings by computing actual confidence intervals from available sample sizes, distinguishing analytical conviction from statistical reliability
components: Sample size assessment for each major claim
Binomial and bootstrap confidence interval computation
Power analysis against gradual vs sudden structural shifts
Separation of descriptive confidence (observable) from predictive confidence (forecasted)
Systematic comparison of stated confidence vs statistically defensible confidence
current_assessment: Most KB confidence ratings are inflated relative to statistical reliability. GPT diffusion lag (N=5-6) has 95% CI spanning ~53pp. Value capture (N=6) spans ~49pp. Equity-credit sequencing (N=3-6) spans 55-72pp. The research process needs to formally distinguish analytical conviction from statistical reliability in every confidence rating. TFP structural break tests have only 20-40% power against gradual shifts.
limitations: Cannot replace domain expertise and analytical judgment
Some claims are grounded in accounting identities or physics, not sample statistics
Low power against gradual shifts means absence of evidence is not evidence of absence
Does not address systematic biases in analogue selection
